title: Intermediaries, Hop-by-Hop Fields, Forwarding, and Transformation · Language Lens: Message Transformation Metadata
summary: A proxy forwards Connection-nominated fields, copies raw forwarding claims, changes content without metadata repair, and assumes downstream and upstream share one connection and parser. Planned artifact: Add and verify the message transformation metadata increment in intermediary forwarding and transformation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Update framing, validators, digests, content codings, warnings, and signatures when an intermediary transforms content.
Model downstream and upstream connections, framing, versions, deadlines, addresses, TLS, and failures as independent protocol hops.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Message Transformation Metadata · Theory Message Transformation Metadata
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1497 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Message Transformation Metadata contract for the supplied data.
